entry.title: Input-triggered transaction expiry
entry.summary:
The concept of "Input expiry" in blockchain transactions can be effectively explained through the mechanics of `nLockTime` and `nSequence`. The parameter `nLockTime` is crucial as it determines the minimum block height at which a transaction may be included in the blockchain. This setting essentially specifies when a transaction becomes eligible for inclusion in the mined blocks, thus playing a pivotal role in timing the execution of transactions.
On the other hand, `nSequence` offers a slightly different utility by dictating how many blocks before a transaction's possible inclusion it expires. This feature enables a transaction input to have an expiration condition independent from the overall transaction expiry, providing enhanced flexibility and control over transaction validity.
These two parameters together frame a comprehensive understanding of input expiry. While they contribute to similar ends—managing the lifecycle of a transaction—their functions are distinct yet complementary. This orthogonal relationship between `nLockTime` and `nSequence` facilitates diverse applications and scenarios in blockchain operations, making their implementation a critical aspect for developers and engineers working with time-sensitive or conditional transactions.
